name: delete_event
description:
Delete an event from the user's connected Google Calendar.

RECURRING EVENTS: When search results show a recurring_event_id, each result is a single occurrence with its own event_id. To delete only ONE occurrence (e.g. just this Wednesday), use that occurrence's event_id. To delete the ENTIRE recurring series, use the recurring_event_id (the master event's ID).

Parameters:
- event_id: The event ID to delete (required, you may need to search to get it). For recurring events: use the instance event_id to delete just that occurrence, or the recurring_event_id to delete the whole series.
- send_updates: "all", "externalOnly", or "none" (default). Only set to "all" if the user explicitly asks to notify attendees, or if you believe notifications are important for this cancellation.
- calendar_id: Optional calendar ID: use to delete an event on a specific calendar. A calendar_id is in email format: people's email addresses correspond to their primary calendars. Defaults to the user's primary calendar.

event_id.description: The event ID to delete (from a previous search result).

send_updates.description: Whether to send email notifications to attendees about the cancellation: "all", "externalOnly", or "none" (default). Only set to "all" if the user explicitly asks to notify attendees.

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID of the event (e.g. email address from google_calendar_list_calendars). Defaults to the user's primary calendar.
